sprint: Atmosphere Engine V1 (MLAS) — Real Foundation Assets Supplied
task: Closes the one disclosed gap the AudioManager implementation (Sprint 2.83.0) shipped with: all five real ElevenLabs-generated Foundation tracks landed and are now wired into assets/audio/foundation/. Each is a genuine, distinct 30.024-second clip (confirmed via ffprobe -- right at the generation cap the whole system was designed around, and via md5sum -- five different files, not duplicates sharing a byte count). One naming mismatch was resolved on arrival: the fifth track was supplied as breath.mp3, not wind.mp3 as every prior design doc/README/code table already named it -- copied in as wind.mp3 to match the filename already established in js/audioManager.js's FOUNDATION_LAYERS table, so zero code change was needed. Verified via a real Playwright pass (not just a file-existence check): all five decode correctly in a real browser (canplaythrough fires, duration===30 for every file, matching ffprobe exactly) and AudioManager's own unlock-listener path fires them all on a real user gesture with zero console/page errors. assets/audio/foundation/README.md updated to drop its own "these files don't exist yet" disclosure. FOUNDATION_LAYERS' own placeholder mix (0.5/0.4/0.3/0.35/0.3) is deliberately left untouched -- re-tuning it by ear needs a human actually listening to the five real files mixed together, which this environment has no way to do; that step remains open, disclosed future work. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to the five real audio files under assets/audio/foundation/ and one README update.
